_notice: SYNTHETIC EXAMPLE DATA — not real cases, not calibration evidence. Every case_id and description below is a constructed illustration proving golden_set_calibrate() works mechanically. See README.md in this directory before treating any number derived from this file as validated.

cases.description: Synthetic example: a feature where all three layers are at least Instrumented.

layers.evidence_summary: Illustrative: task success rate logged weekly against a fixed target.
Illustrative: adoption rate logged monthly against a fixed target.
Illustrative: attributed business impact logged quarterly against a fixed target.

cases.description: Synthetic example: strong model and adoption, zero business metric ever defined.

layers.evidence_summary: Illustrative: reviewed on a fixed cadence, has influenced a model-selection decision.
Illustrative: reviewed on a fixed cadence, has influenced a UX decision.
Illustrative: no business metric was ever attached to this feature's roadmap justification.

cases.description: Synthetic example: strong internal model metrics, override rate past the risk threshold.

layers.evidence_summary: Illustrative: reviewed on a fixed cadence, has influenced a model-selection decision.
Illustrative: override rate observed once in a support review, not logged systematically.
Illustrative: cost-to-serve delta logged monthly against a fixed target.

cases.description: Synthetic example: strong model layer, shallow business tracking, product behaviour untracked entirely.

layers.evidence_summary: Illustrative: feeds a defined retraining trigger, evidenced to have fired at least once.
Illustrative: no product behaviour indicator has ever been logged for this feature.
Illustrative: a business metric appears on a quarterly slide but has no defined target or owner.
